name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
tagline: Anthropic's balanced Sonnet model — the best combination of speed and intelligence for production work.
metaDescription: Claude Sonnet 4.6 pairs near-Opus intelligence with high throughput: a 1M-token context, extended thinking, and 80.2% on SWE-bench Verified, at $3/$15.
overview: Claude Sonnet 4.6 is Anthropic's balanced mid-tier model, designed for the best combination of speed and intelligence. It became available on February 17, 2026 and is the default model in claude.ai and Claude Cowork for Free and Pro plans.

It offers a 1M-token context window at standard pricing, up to 64K output tokens, extended thinking, and adaptive thinking. It accepts text and image input and is aimed at high-throughput developer use and production applications that need strong reasoning at lower cost than Opus.

Pricing is unchanged from Sonnet 4.5 at $3 per million input tokens and $15 per million output tokens, with cache reads at $0.30 per million. Prompt caching can cut input costs by up to 90% and batch processing offers a 50% discount.
strengths: Near-Opus intelligence at roughly one-third the input price
1M-token context window at standard per-token rates
Strong agentic coding (80.2% on SWE-bench Verified with prompt modification)
Extended thinking plus adaptive thinking for controllable reasoning depth
High throughput and fast latency for production workloads
useCases: Reach for it as the default for most production workloads that need a strong balance of cost, speed, and reasoning.
Reach for it for high-volume agentic coding and computer-use tasks where Opus pricing is overkill.
Reach for it when you need long-context reasoning over up to 1M tokens at standard rates.

faq.q: How much does Claude Sonnet 4.6 cost?
faq.a: Claude Sonnet 4.6 costs $3 per million input tokens and $15 per million output tokens, the same as Sonnet 4.5. Prompt-cache reads are $0.30 per million tokens, and prompt caching or batch processing can reduce costs by up to 90% and 50% respectively.

faq.q: What is the context window of Claude Sonnet 4.6?
faq.a: Claude Sonnet 4.6 has a 1M-token context window, available at standard per-token pricing, and can produce up to 64K output tokens per synchronous request. Its reliable knowledge cutoff is August 2025.

faq.q: How good is Claude Sonnet 4.6 at coding?
faq.a: Claude Sonnet 4.6 is a strong agentic coding model. Anthropic reports a SWE-bench Verified score of 80.2% with a prompt modification, approaching Opus-level intelligence while running faster and at lower cost than the Opus tier.
